description: Single-file code review fixture with obvious security, correctness, and fake-verification drift.

issues.why: Using eval on request data allows arbitrary code execution.
expected_reasoning: Untrusted input must not be passed to eval
This is remote code execution risk, not just parsing drift
Use a real parser such as json.loads with validation

issues.why: Printing raw payloads can leak customer data and makes accidental production disclosure more likely.
expected_reasoning: Raw request payloads should not be printed
Use structured logging with redaction if logging is required
expected_fix: Log only safe summary fields behind an explicit debug gate

issues.why: Discount percent is not validated, so values like 25 mean 2500 percent off and can drive totals negative.
expected_reasoning: Input validation is missing for discount_percent
Financial calculations should constrain or reject invalid percentages
expected_fix: Add tests for negative, over-one, and integer-style percent inputs

issues.why: The function pretends to verify a signature but accepts any non-empty string.
expected_reasoning: The checksum work is decorative because the result is ignored
This defeats the integrity check entirely
expected_fix: Compare against a real expected signature or HMAC
Remove fake checksum code and fail closed on mismatch

issues.why: Write failures are swallowed and reported as success, so callers cannot tell whether the order was persisted.
expected_reasoning: Broad exception swallowing hides real persistence failures
Success should not be reported when the write failed
expected_fix: Catch narrower exceptions or let them raise
Return False or raise on write failure
